danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based hostile entities in level layouts
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Platform obstacles, pit geometry, collision-based stage design
danger_signals.meaning: Physical dangers from level structure itself

safety_signals.visual_form: Open platforming sections with no visible enemies or hazards

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban dystopian level settings derived from film source material

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types distinguished by sprite design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Street-level and industrial environments indicating criminal activity zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprites positioned throughout level screen space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol or attack; player must engage in platforming combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive level design from 0-14 with increasing spatial complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density and platforming challenge increases across levels

environmental_storytelling.example: Game based on RoboCop 2 film; urban environments communicate dystopian crime scenario from movie narrative
15 selectable levels (0-14) suggest distinct urban locations progressing through game world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Platforming movement, combat engagement with enemies
Sprite-based movement patterns indicating hostile engagement

notes: Source material is extremely limited—TCRF article focuses on technical/development aspects (level select codes, anti-piracy, hidden text) rather than visual communication design. Game is port of Commodore 64 version based on 1991 RoboCop 2 film. No detailed visual communication documentation available in provided source. Manifestation patterns are inferred from action platformer paradigm and film-adaptation context. NES hardware constraints (sprite-based graphics, limited color palette) heavily influence all visual communication methods.
